title: Generate Parentheses
description: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses. A combination is considered well-formed if every opening parenthesis has a corresponding closing parenthesis.

examples.explanation: For n = 3, we can create the following combinations: 1. '((()))' has three open and three close parentheses. 2. '(()())' has two open followed by one open then close, and so forth. All combinations maintain the balance of parentheses.
For n = 2, the valid combinations are: 1. '(())' which has one set of outer parentheses containing an inner set. 2. '()()' which represents two separate valid pairs of parentheses.
With only one pair of parentheses, the only valid combination is '()'. It is straightforward as there's only one possibility.

constraints: The output will not exceed 5000 combinations.
hints: Consider using a recursive approach to build the parentheses string.
Track the count of opened and closed parentheses during generation.
Ensure that you only add a closing parenthesis if it won't exceed the number of opening ones.
Think about the base case when you have used all pairs of parentheses.
